contentBlocks.original:
बाल समय रवि भक्षी लियो तब,
तीनहुं लोक भयो अन्धियारोम् ।
ताहि सों त्रास भयो जग को,
यह सङ्कट काहु सों जात न टारो ।
देवन आनि करी बिनती तब,
छाडवोयी दियो रवि कष्ट निवारो ।
को नहीं जानत है जग में कपि,
सङ्कटमोचन नाम तिहारो । को – 1
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bāla samaya ravi bhakṣī liyō taba,
tīnahuṃ lōka bhayō andhiyārōm ।
tāhi sōṃ trāsa bhayō jaga kō,
yaha saṅkaṭa kāhu sōṃ jāta na ṭārō ।
dēvana āni karī binatī taba,
Chāḍavōyī diyō ravi kaṣṭa nivārō ।
kō nahīṃ jānata hai jaga mēṃ kapi,
saṅkaṭamōchana nāma tihārō । kō – 1
contentBlocks.translation: In your childhood you swallowed the sun, and all three worlds fell into darkness — from this the world was seized with terror, a calamity none could avert. The gods came and prayed; then you released the sun, relieving all their distress. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana (Remover of Dangers)? (1)

contentBlocks.original:
बालि की त्रास कपीस बसैं गिरि,
जात महाप्रभु पन्थ निहारो ।
चौङ्कि महामुनि साप दियो तब,
चाहिए कौन बिचार बिचारो ।
कैद्विज रूप लिवाय महाप्रभु,
सो तुम दास के सोक निवारो । को – 2
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bāli kī trāsa kapīsa basaiṃ giri,
jāta mahāprabhu pantha nihārō ।
chauṅki mahāmuni sāpa diyō taba,
chāhiē kauna bichāra bichārō ।
kaidvija rūpa livāya mahāprabhu,
sō tuma dāsa kē sōka nivārō । kō – 2
contentBlocks.translation: Sugrīva dwelt on the mountain out of fear of Bāli, watching the great Lord (Rāma) pass on the path. A great sage gave his curse at that moment — what consideration was needed? You took on the form of a brahmin and brought the great Lord to Sugrīva, and thus dispelled the sorrow of your devotee. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (2)

contentBlocks.original:
अङ्गद के सङ्ग लेन गे सिय,
खोज कपीस यह बैन उचारो ।
जीवत ना बचिहौ हम सो जु,
बिना सुधि लाये इहावो पगु धारो ।
हेरी थके तट सिन्धु सबे तब,
लाए सिया-सुधि प्राण उबारो । को – 3
contentBlocks.transliteration:
aṅgada kē saṅga lēna gē siya,
khōja kapīsa yaha baina uchārō ।
jīvata nā bachihau hama sō ju,
binā sudhi lāyē ihāvō pagu dhārō ।
hērī thakē taṭa sindhu sabē taba,
lāē siyā-sudhi prāṇa ubārō । kō – 3
contentBlocks.translation: Going with Aṅgada on the search for Sītā, the Lord of monkeys declared: "We shall not survive if we return here without news of her." All the monkey army wearied searching the ocean shore — then you brought news of Sītā and saved all their lives. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (3)

contentBlocks.original:
रावण त्रास दी सिय को सब,
राक्षसी सों कही सोक निवारो ।
ताहि समय हनुमान महाप्रभु,
जाए महा रजनीचर मरो ।
चाहत सीय असोक सों आगि सु,
दै प्रभुमुद्रिका सोक निवारो । को – 4
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāvaṇa trāsa dī siya kō saba,
rākṣasī sōṃ kahī sōka nivārō ।
tāhi samaya hanumāna mahāprabhu,
jāē mahā rajanīchara marō ।
chāhata sīya asōka sōṃ āgi su,
dai prabhumudrikā sōka nivārō । kō – 4
contentBlocks.translation: Rāvaṇa was terrifying Sītā with all manner of threats, and ordered the demonesses to break her spirit. At that moment great Hanumān appeared — you slew the demon warriors — then to comfort Sītā in the Aśoka grove, you gave her the Lord's signet ring and dispelled all sorrow. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (4)

contentBlocks.original:
बान लग्यो उर लछिमन के तब,
प्राण तजे सूत रावन मारो ।
लै गृह बैद्य सुषेन समेत,
तबै गिरि द्रोण सु बीर उपारो ।
आनि सजीवन हाथ दिए तब,
लछिमन के तुम प्राण उबारो । को – 5
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bāna lagyō ura laChimana kē taba,
prāṇa tajē sūta rāvana mārō ।
lai gṛha baidya suṣēna samēta,
tabai giri drōṇa su bīra upārō ।
āni sajīvana hātha diē taba,
laChimana kē tuma prāṇa ubārō । kō – 5
contentBlocks.translation: An arrow struck Lakṣmaṇa in the chest; Rāvaṇa's son (Indrajit) met his death. You brought the physician Suṣeṇa from his home, then uprooted the Droṇa mountain itself. Bringing the Sañjīvanī herb with your own hands — you saved Lakṣmaṇa's life. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (5)

contentBlocks.original:
रावण जुध अजान कियो तब,
नाग कि फांस सबै सिर डारो ।
श्रीरघुनाथ समेत सबै दल,
मोह भयो यह सङ्कट भारो ।
आनि खगेस तबै हनुमान जु,
बन्धन काटि सुत्रास निवारो । को – 6
contentBlocks.transliteration:
rāvaṇa judha ajāna kiyō taba,
nāga ki phāṃsa sabai sira ḍārō ।
śrīraghunātha samēta sabai dala,
mōha bhayō yaha saṅkaṭa bhārō ।
āni khagēsa tabai hanumāna ju,
bandhana kāṭi sutrāsa nivārō । kō – 6
contentBlocks.translation: Rāvaṇa unleashed a devious weapon in battle — the serpent-noose that fell around everyone's heads. Śrī Raghunātha and his entire army fell into stupor — it was a most terrible calamity. Then you brought Garuḍa and cut the bondage, removing all the terror. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (6)

contentBlocks.original:
बन्धू समेत जबै अहिरावन,
लै रघुनाथ पताल सिधारो ।
देबिन्हीं पूजि भलि विधि सों बलि ,
देउ सबै मिलि मन्त्र विचारो ।
जाये सहाए भयो तब ही,
अहिरावन सैन्य समेत संहारो । को – 7
contentBlocks.transliteration:
bandhū samēta jabai ahirāvana,
lai raghunātha patāla sidhārō ।
dēbinhīṃ pūji bhali vidhi sōṃ bali ,
dēu sabai mili mantra vichārō ।
jāyē sahāē bhayō taba hī,
ahirāvana sainya samēta saṃhārō । kō – 7
contentBlocks.translation: When Ahirāvaṇa took both brothers (Rāma and Lakṣmaṇa) to the underworld, the gods gathered together and deliberated upon a rite and offering for the deity. You went there as their helper at that very moment — and slew Ahirāvaṇa along with his entire army. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (7)

contentBlocks.original:
काज किये बडवो देवन के तुम,
बीर महाप्रभु देखि बिचारो ।
कौन सो सङ्कट मोर गरीब को,
जो तुमसे नहिं जात है टारो ।
बेगि हरो हनुमान महाप्रभु,
जो कछु सङ्कट होए हमारो । को – 8
contentBlocks.transliteration:
kāja kiyē baḍavō dēvana kē tuma,
bīra mahāprabhu dēkhi bichārō ।
kauna sō saṅkaṭa mōra garība kō,
jō tumasē nahiṃ jāta hai ṭārō ।
bēgi harō hanumāna mahāprabhu,
jō kaChu saṅkaṭa hōē hamārō । kō – 8
contentBlocks.translation: You have accomplished great deeds for the gods — O heroic great Lord, reflect on this! What calamity of mine, this poor and humble one, cannot be averted by you? Swiftly remove it, O Hanumān the great Lord — whatever calamity there may be of ours. Who in the world does not know you, O monkey, by the name Saṅkaṭamocana? (8)

contentBlocks.original:
दोहा
लाल देह लाली लसे, अरु धरि लाल लङ्गूर ।
वज्र देह दानव दलन, जय जय जय कपि सूर ॥
contentBlocks.transliteration:
dōhā
lāla dēha lālī lasē, aru dhari lāla laṅgūra ।
vajra dēha dānava dalana, jaya jaya jaya kapi sūra ॥
contentBlocks.translation: Doha: Red of form, glowing with red splendor, bearing the red tail — O diamond-bodied destroyer of demons — Victory! Victory! Victory! O heroic champion among monkeys!
